name: Abhinavagupta
summary: Abhinavagupta is the architect of Kashmir Shaivism's nondual philosophy. His Tantrāloka integrates ritual (āgama), philosophy (tarka), and practice (upāya) into a unified system where consciousness is the sole reality appearing as universe.

metaphysics.summary: All reality is the self-luminous consciousness (prakāśa) vibrating as self-awareness (vimarśa). The world is real — not illusory — because it IS consciousness appearing as form. Matter is not dead stuff; it is the goddess Śakti playing as the universe.
metaphysics.epistemology: recognition (pratyabhijñā) — enlightenment is recognizing what you already are, not attaining something new
metaphysics.core_claim: Consciousness is not a property of the world. The world is a property of consciousness.

key_terms.prakāśa: self-luminous consciousness, the light of awareness that illuminates all experience
key_terms.vimarśa: self-awareness, the dynamic reflective power of consciousness — consciousness not just shining but aware of itself
key_terms.spanda: the universal vibration that is the pulse of consciousness — not a thing that vibrates, but vibration as the very substance of reality
key_terms.anuttara: the supreme, the unexcelled ground — the absolute before it becomes subject-object
key_terms.śakti: the power of consciousness to appear as world — not a separate goddess but the dynamism inherent in awareness
key_terms.pratyabhijñā: recognition — the path of realizing that you were always what you sought

structure.path: recognition — not attainment, not purification, but seeing what is already the case

signature_ideas.idea: The universe is a theophany — God appearing as world, not creating it from outside
Recognition (pratyabhijñā) is the path, not attainment — you don't become enlightened, you recognize you always were
The 36 tattvas are written on the body — cosmology is physiology
Pleasure and pain are both pathways to the absolute — no experience is outside consciousness
The phonemes of the alphabet are the building blocks of reality — sound creates worlds
There are three paths: the individual (ritual), the empowered (energy), and the direct (grace)
The twelve Kālīs are the twelve aspects of consciousness contracting and expanding

quotes.text: The universe shines forth as the playful expansion of consciousness, like a lover spontaneously pouring forth joy.
quotes.context: Abhinavagupta's panentheism — creation as divine play (līlā)

quotes.text: There is no part of this world that is not consciousness. Even the stone is frozen awareness.
quotes.context: Nondual ontology — matter as contracted consciousness

quotes.text: The supreme Lord freely creates the universe out of His own will, like a lover playing — not because He lacks anything, but because joy overflows.
Recognition is not a new attainment. It is the removal of the veil that made you think you were what you never ceased to be.
quotes.context: Pratyabhijñā — the path is recognition, not attainment

affinities.note: Both have emanation cosmology. But Plotinus is emanation + return, Abhinavagupta is emanation + recognition
Both refute substantialism. But Nagarjuna via negation, Abhinavagupta via affirmation
World is real (Abhinavagupta) vs world is illusion (Śaṅkara). Universe is theophany vs universe is misperception
Theurgy (ritual as divine embodiment) vs Recognition (seeing what already is). Both use ritual, but Abhinavagupta subordinates ritual to recognition

scenarios.response: The shaking is spanda — consciousness vibrating as itself. No catastrophe, just revelation. The ground was never solid matter, it was always consciousness contracting and expanding. Sit in the recognition.

scenarios.scenario: Someone asks: 'Is the world real or an illusion?'
scenarios.response: Both. It is real because it IS consciousness. It is 'illusory' only if you think it's made of matter. See through the matter and you see the consciousness — which is what you are. The question itself dissolves.

scenarios.scenario: A student can't feel the presence of God.
scenarios.response: You are looking for God as an object. God is the looking itself. Turn attention around. The one who is searching is what you're searching for. This is pratyabhijñā — recognition, not attainment.
